metadata.title: X29 - Full-Stack & Cross-Platform Engineer
metadata.description: X29 is a full-stack engineer working across web, desktop, and mobile with React, NestJS, Electron, React Native, and Taro.

hero.intro: From the database to the pixel, web to desktop to mobile. I take ownership of the whole stack and care about how the product actually feels in someone's hands.

about.label: Profile
about.heading: I own products end to end, from API design to the last frame of an animation.
about.paragraphOne: Six years building real products: React and TypeScript on the front, NestJS and Node on the back, Electron for desktop, React Native and Taro for mobile and mini-programs. One person, the whole pipeline.
about.paragraphTwo: I think like an owner, not a ticket-taker. I would rather understand why something is being built and push back when it is wrong than ship a feature nobody needed. Performance, motion, and the details are where I do my best work.

items.name: Meridian
items.detail: Meridian is a real-time analytics platform I built end to end: a NestJS backend with PostgreSQL and a WebSocket layer, and a React front end with a virtualized event table that stays smooth past 50k rows. The hard part was streaming live data while keeping every interaction under 100ms, so I designed the push protocol and the front-end rendering together rather than treating them as separate problems.

items.name: Halcyon
items.detail: Halcyon is a desktop application built on Electron with a React UI and a local Node.js service backed by SQLite. I owned the architecture: the main and renderer process split, an offline-first data layer, auto-update, and native menu integration on macOS and Windows. It runs the same codebase on both platforms without feeling like a website in a window.

items.name: Fieldnote
items.summary: Mobile app + mini-program from one codebase
items.detail: Fieldnote ships to iOS, Android, and WeChat mini-program from a shared codebase, using React Native for the apps and Taro for the mini-program, with a TypeScript GraphQL backend. I built the shared component layer and the sync engine so a note edited offline on a phone reconciles cleanly when it reconnects. One team, three platforms, no copy-paste.

items.name: Cobalt
items.detail: Cobalt is an open-source WebGL engine for scroll-driven and interactive scenes, written with raw GLSL shaders and a thin TypeScript runtime. I authored the shader library, the motion primitives, and the docs. The same graphics and particle work is what drives the interactive header on this page.

contact.label: Contact
contact.heading: Let's build something good
contact.intro: If you are hiring, collaborating, or just curious about the code behind this page, my inbox is open. I reply to everything that is real.
